title: kiewia teijsmannii
intro: Kiewia teijsmannii, also known as Kiewia, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, classified as a type of flowering plant in the family Burseraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Kiewia teijsmannii has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to combat bronchitis, pneumonia, and tuberculosis due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Kiewia teijsmannii can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which have been reported to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and improving respiratory function.
